decisions.what: Extended User.role union with UserRole enum values instead of replacing the existing 'client' | 'influencer' | 'admin' union
decisions.why: Preserves backwards compatibility with existing code referencing legacy role values while introducing the new EMPLOYER/EMPLOYEE roles required by the spec.

decisions.what: Kept handwritten validators in lib/validations/auth.ts rather than using zod
decisions.why: The file's header note states zod is not in package.json; existing patterns use plain validation functions, so the new role validator follows the same convention.

decisions.what: Treated 'first login' as account age within 24h in use-onboarding
decisions.why: Spec says to detect first login by comparing created_at to the current session; a 24h window is a reasonable fresh-account window and keeps logic deterministic without persisting per-session flags.

decisions.what: Bypassed the welcome modal when user.role is not EMPLOYER/EMPLOYEE
decisions.why: Legacy roles (client/influencer/admin) have no onboarding content defined, so the modal only renders for new EMPLOYER/EMPLOYEE accounts.
Spec explicitly requests PATCH /api/v1/auth/me with { is_onboarded: true }; the apiClient baseURL already includes /api/v1.
